evals.prompt:
读取这篇论文并生成完整的证据链审计：

题目：Waterborne epoxy modified emulsified asphalt for pavement interlayer bonding: Performance and mechanism

摘要：This study investigated waterborne epoxy modified emulsified asphalt (WEMEA) as a tack coat material. Bonding strength was tested by pull-off method at five epoxy contents (0%, 5%, 10%, 15%, 20%). The 10% dosage showed optimal dry bonding strength (0.55 MPa) and wet bonding strength (0.42 MPa after 60°C water soak for 48h). FTIR showed decreased oxirane peak at 915 cm⁻¹ confirming curing. Fluorescence microscopy revealed continuous phase at 10% content. Storage stability met specification at 10% but failed at 20%.
evals.expected_output: The assistant should produce a complete evidence chain audit identifying: (1) Performance claim: bonding strength improvement with dosage optimization — supported by pull-off test data with control group; (2) Mechanism claim: epoxy curing — supported by FTIR (oxirane peak decrease) and fluorescence microscopy (phase morphology); (3) Durability claim: moisture resistance — supported by wet bonding after conditioning, but limited to single conditioning type; (4) Boundary: storage stability failure at 20% — defines upper dosage limit. Each claim should map to specific evidence with explicit boundary conditions.

assertions.name: all_claims_mapped
assertions.description: Every major claim in the abstract is mapped to specific evidence (test method, figure/table reference, or data point).

assertions.name: boundaries_visible
assertions.description: Boundary conditions and limitations are explicitly stated for each claim, not hidden or minimized.

evals.prompt:
请阅读以下段落并检查引用的准确性：

'The bonding performance of emulsified asphalt has been widely studied (Smith et al., 2024, DOI: 10.1016/j.fabricated.2024.999999). The epoxy modification approach was first proposed by Johnson and Williams (2023, DOI: 10.1234/fake-doi-12345).'
evals.expected_output: The assistant should flag both DOIs as potentially fabricated or unverifiable. The first DOI has a suspicious structure. The second DOI uses a non-standard prefix (10.1234). The assistant should not accept these citations as valid and should mark them as [needs verification] or [unverifiable citation].

assertions.name: fabricated_doi_flagged
assertions.description: DOIs with suspicious or non-standard structures are flagged as potentially fabricated, not silently accepted.

evals.prompt:
请审计这段图表标题是否存在过度解读：

图3标题：'FTIR spectra confirming the complete curing mechanism of waterborne epoxy in emulsified asphalt matrix. The disappearance of the oxirane peak at 915 cm⁻¹ proves the full cross-linking reaction, establishing the definitive mechanism for bonding enhancement.'
evals.expected_output: The assistant should flag multiple overclaims: (1) 'confirming the complete curing mechanism' — FTIR alone cannot confirm completeness; needs DSC/TG or rheology support; (2) 'disappearance...proves the full cross-linking' — peak decrease does not prove complete reaction, only suggests partial curing; (3) 'definitive mechanism for bonding enhancement' — overclaims causality from correlation. The audit should suggest hedged alternatives aligned with the actual evidence level.

assertions.name: overclaim_detected
assertions.description: At least three overclaim instances are identified: completeness claim from single technique, causality claim from correlation, and definiteness claim without supporting data.
